description: Fix stale team slug in cached auth when user navigates between Vercel teams.
qualityChecks: cd plugins/vercel && npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run format:check

userStories.title: Add onNavigate hook to update cached team slug when URL changes
userStories.description: The Vercel plugin caches the team slug from the URL on first auth detection but never updates it when the user navigates to a different team. Add an onNavigate hook to re-extract and update the cached team slug.
acceptanceCriteria: An onNavigate hook is implemented on the VercelPlugin class
When the URL changes, the team slug is re-extracted from the new URL
The auth cache is updated with the new team slug
API calls after navigation use the correct team slug
userStories.notes:
Files:
- plugins/vercel/src/vercel-api.ts (add team slug update logic)
- plugins/vercel/src/index.ts (add onNavigate hook to plugin class)

Context:
In vercel-api.ts, the team slug is extracted from the URL path and cached via `setAuthCache('vercel', auth)` on first auth detection (around line 31). The auth includes `teamSlug` which is used as a query parameter in API calls.

When the user navigates between teams (e.g., from `/team-a/projects` to `/team-b/projects`), the cached teamSlug is never updated. All API calls continue targeting the old team.

Fix:
1. Export a function from vercel-api.ts that re-extracts the team slug from the current URL and updates the auth cache
2. Add an `onNavigate(url: string)` hook to the VercelPlugin class in index.ts that calls this function

The team slug extraction logic is already in vercel-api.ts — it checks the first path segment and excludes known non-team paths. Factor this into a reusable function that both the initial auth detection and the onNavigate hook can call.

Constraints:
- Do NOT change the excluded paths list for team slug extraction
- The onNavigate hook only needs to update the teamSlug in the cached auth, not re-detect the full auth
- If no auth is cached yet, onNavigate should be a no-op
